samples.gvd:
module div_8bit (
    input wire clk,
    input wire rst,
    input wire [7:0] dividend,
    input wire [7:0] divisor,
    input wire sign,
    input wire opn_valid,
    output reg res_valid,
    input wire res_ready,
    output reg [15:0] result
);
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. The design supports both signed and unsigned division operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst">The reset signal to initialize or reset the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit the output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. The design supports both signed and unsigned division operations.</implementation>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant name="SIGNED" value="1">Indicates signed operation.</constant>
        <constant name="UNSIGNED" value="0">Indicates unsigned operation.</constant>
        <constant name="DIVISION_DONE" value="8'b10000000">Division done flag.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="dividend_reg" width="8" type="register">Register to hold the dividend.</reg>
        <reg id="divisor_reg" width="8" type="register">Register to hold the divisor.</reg>
        <reg id="shift_register" width="16" type="register">Shift register for division results.</reg>
        <reg id="neg_divisor" width="8" type="register">Negative divisor for subtraction.</reg>
        <reg id="counter" width="8" type="register">Counter for iterations.</reg>
        <reg id="start_counter" width="1" type="register">Start counter flag.</reg>
        <reg id="result_valid" width="1" type="register">Result valid flag.</reg>
        <wire id="carry_out" width="1">Carry out signal from subtraction.</wire>
        <wire id="sub_result" width="9">Subtraction result.</wire>
        <mux id="mux_result" width="16">Multiplexor for selecting result based on carry out.</mux>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Sequential logic block
        always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
            if (rst) begin
                dividend_reg <= 8'b0;
                divisor_reg <= 8'b0;
                shift_register <= 16'b0;
                neg_divisor <= 8'b0;
                counter <= 8'b0;
                start_counter <= 1'b0;
                result_valid <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                if (opn_valid && !result_valid) begin
                    dividend_reg <= dividend;
                    divisor_reg <= divisor;
                    shift_register <= {dividend[7], dividend} << 1; // Shift dividend left by 1
                    neg_divisor <= ~divisor + 1; // Negate divisor
                    counter <= 8'b1;
                    start_counter <= 1'b1;
                end else if (start_counter) begin
                    if (counter == DIVISION_DONE) begin
                        counter <= 8'b0;
                        start_counter <= 1'b0;
                        shift_register <= {shift_register[15:8], carry_out}; // Update shift register
                    end else begin
                        counter <= counter + 1;
                        shift_register <= {shift_register[15:8], carry_out}; // Update shift register
                    end
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Combinational logic block
        always @(*) begin
            if (start_counter) begin
                sub_result = shift_register[15:8] + neg_divisor; // Perform subtraction
                carry_out = sub_result[8]; // Carry out from subtraction
                mux_result = carry_out ? {shift_register[15:8], 8'b0} : {shift_register[15:8], 8'b1}; // Select result based on carry out
            end else begin
                mux_result = shift_register; // No change if not starting
            end
        end
        
        // Output assignment
        assign result = {shift_register[15:8], shift_register[7:0]}; // Combine remainder and quotient
        assign res_valid = rst ? 1'b0 : (counter == DIVISION_DONE || (result_valid && res_ready));
        
        // Result validity management
        always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
            if (rst) begin
                result_valid <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                if (counter == DIVISION_DONE) begin
                    result_valid <= 1'b1;
                end else if (result_valid && res_ready) begin
                    result_valid <= 1'b0;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
